name: Personal Information Protection Act
description: Alberta's private-sector privacy law (SA 2003 c P-6.5). One of three substantially-similar-to-PIPEDA provincial private-sector privacy acts (along with BC PIPA and Quebec's Act respecting the protection of personal information in the private sector).
relationToCloudSecurity: Applies to cloud-hosted systems handling personal information of Albertans within scope.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to AI systems processing personal information within scope.

license.notes: Government publication of Alberta. King's Printer maintains the consolidated text.

lifecycle.notes: In force since 2004; multiple amendments since.

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF from Alberta King's Printer (kings-printer.alberta.ca/documents/Acts/P06P5.pdf), marker-extracted to markdown. extract_alberta_pipa.py produces per-section structured CSV/JSON (75 sections, all of 1-63 plus letter-suffixed amendments).
